findings: Local context indicates a representation cluster where Average Packet Size, Avg Bwd Segment Size, Bwd Packet Length Max, Bwd Packet Length Mean, Bwd Packet Length Std, and Destination Port exhibit strong class-conditioned distribution differences, including high variance ratios, unequal unique_values_by_class, and high JS divergence for port distributions.

A separate dependency cluster indicates strong redundancy among related packet-size, segment-size, subflow, and flag-count features, with multiple near-perfect pairwise correlations and several exact 1.0 relations.
contradictions: Strong linear redundancy coexists with class-conditioned distribution differences, so redundant features may still carry distinct representational patterns.
Some related features are effectively constant while others in the same neighborhood vary strongly by class, leaving ambiguity about whether constants are meaningful signals or artifacts.
limitations: No local action was executed in this worker segment, so this result is based only on provided local context summaries rather than direct verification traces.
Pairwise redundancy and per-feature distribution profiles were not freshly collected here, so the evidence remains contextual rather than procedurally confirmed.
